name: UI
summary: The UI module contains a number of methods for creating simple UI elements from a SketchUp Ruby script.

methods.name: add_context_menu_handler
methods.summary: The add_context_menu_handler method is used to register a block of code with SketchUp that will be called when a context menu is to be displayed.
methods.remarks: The add_context_menu_handler method is used to register a block of code with SketchUp that will be called when a context menu is to be displayed. The context menu handler can then display the context menu with the items that you have added. Be careful with what you do in a context menu handler. If you perform an operation takes take a long time, such as traversing the model or selection in a large model it will delay the menu. See the contextmenu.rb script in the Plugins/examples directory for an example.

returns.doc: the number of context handlers that are registered

methods.name: beep
methods.summary: The beep method plays a system beep sound.
methods.remarks: The beep method plays a system beep sound. The beep method does not accept any arguments nor return any values.

methods.name: create_cursor
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2016 it is possible to provide vector images for the cursors.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2016 it is possible to provide vector images for the cursors. SVG format for Windows and PDF format for macOS. This is the recommended format to use since it will scale well with different DPI scaling. Note: Because the image is never released, avoid creating new cursors for the same image for any given tool. Cache it and reuse it. The create_cursor method is used to create a cursor from an image file at the specified location. This must be called from within a custom tool. See the Sketchup::Tool documentation for a complete example. The size of the cursor images should be 32x32 pixels. Any size different from that will be scaled using the shrink-to-fit policy. The coordinates for the cursor's hotspot are based from it's top left corner, starting at (0, 0). For example, a value of (hot_x, hot_y) = (5, 10) would identify the hotpoint of the cursor at 5 pixels from the left edge of your cursor image and 10 pixels from the top edge of your cursor image.

params.name: path
params.doc: File path to an image.

params.name: hot_x
params.doc: The x-coordinate of the “hotpoint” of the cursor, computed from the left edge of your cursor image.

params.name: hot_y
params.doc: The y-coordinate of the “hotpoint” of the cursor, computed from the top edge of your cursor image.

returns.doc: Id associated with the cursor. Use this with set_cursor in Sketchup::Tool#onSetCursor.

throws.when: if @param hot_x or @param hot_y are negative or larger than the max value of an Integer.

methods.name: get_clipboard_data
methods.summary: Returns the plain text available on the clipboard, if there is any.
methods.remarks: Returns the plain text available on the clipboard, if there is any.

methods.name: inputbox
methods.signature: inputbox(prompts, defaults, title) => Array<String>, false | inputbox(prompts, defaults, list, title) => Array<String>, false
methods.summary: Note: The method intelligently handles various types for default values and lists, automatically attempting to convert provided inputs to match the type of the default value.
methods.remarks: Note: The method intelligently handles various types for default values and lists, automatically attempting to convert provided inputs to match the type of the default value. This ensures consistency in data types throughout the operation. Creates a dialog box for inputting user information. The dialog box contains input fields with static text prompts, optional default values, optional drop down selections, and optional title. You can also use this method to display drop down lists of options, by passing an optional param.

params.name: prompts
params.doc: An array of prompt names appearing in the input box adjacent to input fields.

params.name: defaults
params.doc: An array of default values for the input fields.

params.name: title
params.doc: The title for the input box.

returns.doc: An array of returned values if the user did not cancel the dialog. If the user canceled the dialog, false is returned. The returned values in the array will be in the same order as the input fields.

methods.name: inspector_names
methods.summary: The inspector_names method is used to returns the names of all the inspectors.
methods.remarks: The inspector_names method is used to returns the names of all the inspectors. Inspectors are another name for the various floating dialog windows that you can activate from withing SketchUp, such as the Materials window.

returns.doc: an array of strings containing the names of inspectors.

methods.name: menu
methods.summary: Note: The 'Extensions' menu was named 'Plugins' prior to SketchUp 2015.
methods.remarks: Note: The 'Extensions' menu was named 'Plugins' prior to SketchUp 2015. For backward compatibility 'Plugins' still works. Note: 'Developer' menu was added with SketchUp 2021.1. The menu method retrieves a SketchUp's menu object with a given name. This is the first step toward adding your own custom items to the bottom of SketchUp's menus. Valid menu names are: 'File', 'Edit', 'View', 'Camera', 'Draw', 'Tools', 'Window', 'Extensions', 'Help' and 'Developer'.

params.name: menu_name
params.doc: The name of a supported menu.

methods.name: messagebox
methods.summary: Creates a dialog box containing static text with a series of buttons for the user to choose from.
methods.remarks: Creates a dialog box containing static text with a series of buttons for the user to choose from. Valid message box types are: MB_OK - Contains an OK button. MB_OKCANCEL - Contains OK and Cancel buttons. MB_ABORTRETRYIGNORE - Contains Abort, Retry, and Ignore buttons. MB_YESNOCANCEL - Contains Yes, No, and Cancel buttons. MB_YESNO - Contains Yes and No buttons. MB_RETRYCANCEL - Contains Retry and Cancel buttons. MB_MULTILINE - Contains and OK button. Return values can be any of following: IDOK IDCANCEL IDABORT IDRETRY IDIGNORE IDYES IDNO In an MB_MULTILINE message box, the message is displayed as a multi-line message with scrollbars (as needed). MB_MULTILNE also allows a third string argument that will be used as the title for the messagebox.

params.name: message
params.doc: The message that you want to display.

params.name: type
params.doc: The message box type, which will be a constant from the list in the method comments.

returns.doc: A number corresponding to what the user selected.

methods.name: model_info_pages
methods.summary: The model_info_pages method is used to returns the names of all the available model info pages.
methods.remarks: The model_info_pages method is used to returns the names of all the available model info pages. These include UI windows such as Components, Credits, and Units.

returns.doc: an array of strings containing the names of model info pages.

methods.name: openpanel
methods.summary: The openpanel method is used to display the Open dialog box.
methods.remarks: The openpanel method is used to display the Open dialog box. The path that is returned can then be used inside code to open a text or image file. See the standard Ruby class File for examples of reading and writing from disk.

params.name: title
params.doc: The title to apply to the open dialog box.

params.name: directory
params.doc: The default directory for the open panel.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The default filename for the open panel. On Windows, you can alternatively pass a wildcard filter using this format: UIname|wildcard||. Additional filter dropdown list items can be added by adding additional pairs of filter name and filter like this: UIname1|wildcard1|UIname2|wildcard2||. Also multiple wildcard filters can be combined into a single line using a semicolon-separated list in the filter field: ui_name|wildcard1;wildcard2||.

returns.doc: the full path and name of the file selected, or nil if the dialog was canceled.

methods.name: openURL
methods.summary: The openURL method is used to open the default browser to a URL.
methods.remarks: The openURL method is used to open the default browser to a URL.

methods.name: play_sound
methods.summary: The play_sound method is used to play a sound file.
methods.remarks: The play_sound method is used to play a sound file. Valid sound files include .wav and .mp3 files on the Mac and .wav files on the PC.

params.name: filename
params.doc: the relative path to the filename from the SketchUp install directory, or an absolute path to the file. (See Sketchup.find_support_file for a way to search for a specific file.)

methods.name: preferences_pages
methods.summary: The preferences_pages method is used to returns the names of all the preferences pages.
methods.remarks: The preferences_pages method is used to returns the names of all the preferences pages. These include windows like Templates. SketchUp 2017 "Extensions" page was removed.

returns.doc: an array of strings containing the names of preference pages.

methods.name: refresh_inspectors
methods.summary: Tells SketchUp to refresh all inspectors such as the Component Browser and the Outliner.
methods.remarks: Tells SketchUp to refresh all inspectors such as the Component Browser and the Outliner. This is useful when you need to manually force a refresh after you've made a change to the document via Ruby. Generally, SketchUp will keep these in sync for you, but occasionally it does not, such as when model.start_operation has disabled UI updates.

methods.name: refresh_toolbars
methods.summary: Tells SketchUp to refresh all floating toolbars.
methods.remarks: Tells SketchUp to refresh all floating toolbars. This is useful when you need to manually force a refresh after you've made a change to the document via Ruby. Generally, SketchUp will keep these in sync for you, but occasionally it does not, such as when Sketchup::Model#start_operation has disabled UI updates. This only affects macOS, on Windows the toolbars are always refreshing.

methods.name: savepanel
methods.summary: The savepanel method is used to display the Save dialog box.
methods.remarks: The savepanel method is used to display the Save dialog box. The path that is returned can then be used inside code to save out a text or image file. See the standard Ruby class File for examples of reading and writing from disk.

params.name: title
params.doc: The title to apply to the save dialog box.

params.name: directory
params.doc: The default directory for the save panel.

params.name: filename
params.doc: The default filename for the save panel. On Windows, you can alternatively pass a mask, like “*.txt”, to have all the .txt files display. If you want multiple file types to display, you can supply multiple masks for the filename and separate them with a semicolon, like this: “.txt;.doc”.

returns.doc: the full path and name of the file selected or nil if the dialog was canceled.

methods.name: scale_factor
methods.summary: Note: SU2017M0 will automatically scale up line width and text size, but will not scale up the points provided to Sketchup::View#draw2d.
methods.remarks: Note: SU2017M0 will automatically scale up line width and text size, but will not scale up the points provided to Sketchup::View#draw2d. Returns the scaling factor SketchUp uses on high DPI monitors. Useful for things like Sketchup::View#draw2d.

methods.name: select_directory
methods.summary: The select_directory method is used to display the OS dialog for selecting one or several directories from the file system.
methods.remarks: The select_directory method is used to display the OS dialog for selecting one or several directories from the file system.

params.name: options
params.doc: The dialog can be customized by providing a hash or named arguments of options.

returns.doc: A string with the full path of the directory selected when :select_multiple option is set to false otherwise an array of strings or nil if the user cancelled.

methods.name: set_clipboard_data
methods.summary: Copies plain text to the clipboard.
methods.remarks: Copies plain text to the clipboard.

methods.name: set_cursor
methods.summary: The set_cursor method is used to change the cursor to a new cursor with a given cursor id.
methods.remarks: The set_cursor method is used to change the cursor to a new cursor with a given cursor id. See UI.create_cursor and the Tool class for details on creating your own tools with arbitrary cursors. If you call this while a standard SketchUp tool is active, you will not see your custom cursor, as these tools are constantly setting their own cursors to indicate SketchUp's state.

params.name: cursor_id
params.doc: The id of the cursor you want to display.

methods.name: set_toolbar_visible
methods.summary: The set_toolbar_visible method is used to set whether a given toolbar is visible.
methods.remarks: The set_toolbar_visible method is used to set whether a given toolbar is visible. Note that the toolbars and their names are different on the Mac vs. PC, so be careful and be sure to test when using this method in a cross-platform script.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of a Ruby toolbar.

params.name: visible
params.doc: True to make the toolbar visible, false to hide it.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if not.

methods.name: show_extension_manager
methods.summary: The show_extension_manager method is used to display the Extension Manager dialog.
methods.remarks: The show_extension_manager method is used to display the Extension Manager dialog.

methods.name: show_extension_warehouse
methods.summary: Show the Extension Warehouse dialog inside SketchUp.
methods.remarks: Show the Extension Warehouse dialog inside SketchUp. If an extension UUID is provided the dialog navigates directly to that extension's page. If no UUID is provided it opens the Extension Warehouse home page.

params.name: extension_id
params.doc: Optional Extension Warehouse UUID. If nil or omitted the EW home page is shown.

methods.name: show_inspector
methods.summary: The show_inspector method is used to display the inspector with the given name.
methods.remarks: The show_inspector method is used to display the inspector with the given name. You can get the list of valid inspectors with UI.inspector_names.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of inspector that you want to display.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: show_model_info
methods.summary: The show_model_info method is used to display the model info dialog for a specific page.
methods.remarks: The show_model_info method is used to display the model info dialog for a specific page. You can get the list of valid page names with model_info_pages. SketchUp 2014 "Classifications" page was added.

params.name: page_name
params.doc: The name of the model info dialog you want to display.

methods.name: show_preferences
methods.summary: The show_preferences method is used to display a SketchUp preferences dialog.
methods.remarks: The show_preferences method is used to display a SketchUp preferences dialog. You can get the list of valid dialogs with preferences_pages.

params.name: page_name
params.doc: The name of the preferences dialog you want to display.

methods.name: start_timer
methods.summary: The start_timer method is used to start a timer.
methods.remarks: The start_timer method is used to start a timer. This is an effective method to create a repeating snippet of code for arbitrary animation. See this blog post for an detailed example of custom animation using timers: sketchupapi.blogspot.com/2008/10/animate-yo-cheese.html Note that there is a bug that if you open a modal window in a non-repeating timer the timer will repeat until the window is closed.

params.name: seconds
params.doc: The time in seconds before your code should be called.

params.name: repeat
params.doc: true if you want the timer to repeat, false (or omit) if you do not want it to repeat.

methods.name: stop_timer
methods.summary: The stop_timer method is used to stop a timer based on its id.
methods.remarks: The stop_timer method is used to stop a timer based on its id.

params.name: id
params.doc: The timer id for the timer that you want to stop.

methods.name: toolbar
methods.summary: The toolbar method is used to get a Ruby toolbar by name.
methods.remarks: The toolbar method is used to get a Ruby toolbar by name. If the toolbar doesn't exist a new one will be created.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the Ruby toolbar.

methods.name: toolbar_names
methods.summary: The toolbar_names method is used to return the name of all the available native toolbars (this differs between PC and Mac).
methods.remarks: The toolbar_names method is used to return the name of all the available native toolbars (this differs between PC and Mac). These toolbar names do not include Ruby toolbars.

returns.doc: Array of strings representing toolbar names.

methods.name: toolbar_visible?
methods.summary: The toolbar_visible? method is used to determine whether a given toolbar is visible.
methods.remarks: The toolbar_visible? method is used to determine whether a given toolbar is visible. Note that the toolbars and their names are different on the Mac vs. PC, so be careful and be sure to test when using this method in a cross-platform script.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of a native toolbar.
